patches.summary: Drop 'Digg AI' / 'Digg AI 1000' / '1,000 AI accounts' product branding from prose surfaces; the CLI is now called 'Digg'.
patches.reason: Upstream Digg's AI-news leaderboard now markets itself as plain 'Digg' (with /ai/ as the URL prefix). The 'AI 1000' framing was inherited from an earlier iteration of the product. Keeping it in display strings was confusing users.
patches.validated_outcome: agent-context, --help, and SKILL.md frontmatter all now read 'Digg' and reference the four GitHub feeds.
patches.scope_note: Internal code identifiers (roster_1000.go, the /ai/1000 endpoint path, ai_1000_fixture.html) are left alone — they describe the upstream endpoint, not user-facing prose.

patches.summary: Add a `github` parent command with four subcommands (stars, new, activity, recent) backed by RSC parsers for the /ai/github/* pages.
patches.reason: Digg surfaces a parallel set of GitHub feeds alongside the X-account leaderboard. The original CLI shipped before those existed; users wanted them exposed as structured JSON.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / go vet / go test all green; each of the four commands returns live structured records when dogfooded (stars: nested {repo,judgment}; new: flat event records; activity: contributor leaderboard with rank/contributions/repos; recent: per-user events paired with github.com URLs).

patches.summary: Add a `rankings` parent command with three subcommands (emerging, movers, list) backed by an RSC parser for /ai/x/rankings/companies, plus a `--min-starrers N` flag on `github stars` for smart-money-convergence filtering. Adds a ParseStats type + --max-skip-ratio schema-drift gate shared by the new commands.
patches.reason: Two high-signal UI surfaces on digg.com weren't exposed by the CLI. (1) /ai/x/rankings/companies has a curated 'EMERGING STARTUPS' list and follower-velocity Movers section that map directly to VC deal-sourcing use cases; the CLI only had the AI 1000 leaderboard at /ai/1000. (2) The github stars feed surfaces a right-rail multi-starrer rollup on the website which is just the same feed filtered by distinct_starrers >= 2; users have no way to get that slice from the CLI without post-processing. Both gaps are addressed by reading existing fields out of the same RSC payloads — no new endpoints, no breaking changes.
patches.validated_outcome: Live smoke against digg.com at PR time: 'rankings emerging' returns 10 entries (Inspired Cognition, Evolving Programs, Shadow Robot, Tavus, etc.); 'rankings movers --direction up' returns 10 gainers (rank 76 aclanthology, rank 4 GoogleAI...); 'rankings list --limit 3' returns OpenAI(#1), GoogleDeepMind(#2), AnthropicAI(#3); 'github stars --min-starrers 2' narrows 114 unfiltered rows to 6 with distinct_starrers >= 2.
patches.scope_note: Rankings leaf commands intentionally OMIT the pp:endpoint annotation. The MCP cobratree walker treats pp:endpoint-tagged commands as 'covered by a typed MCP tool' and skips registering a shell-out tool; until a typed rankings tool ships, the rankings commands need shell-out MCP exposure. The existing github stars/new/activity/recent commands have the same annotation/MCP-skip behavior as a known issue, not as precedent.

patches.summary: Add three small surface enhancements: `sql <query>` read-only passthrough (driver-level mode=ro), `authors overlap <a> <b>` INTERSECT query, and `--topics` multi-topic flag on sync.
patches.reason: Follow-up to the closed digg-ai PR #704. Read-only enforcement for `sql` runs at the SQLite driver via store.OpenReadOnly so writable CTEs (`WITH x AS (DELETE ... RETURNING ...) SELECT * FROM x`) are rejected before parse — the SELECT/WITH prefix check is just a friendlier error path.
patches.validated_outcome: go build / go test all green; smoke: writable-CTE rejected by driver mode=ro; `authors overlap karpathy sama --json` returns INTERSECT result; `sync --topics ai,technology --dry-run` reports both topics; `RecordReplacementsForTopics` scopes the 2-hour window query to the synced topics via WHERE topic IN (...) so a topic-subset sync no longer pollutes the replacements table.
